channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: STRC now pays 11.5% dividend. — 31 replies — 124.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
STRC now pays 11.5% dividend.

Open thread • Replies: 31 • Updated: 2026-03-03 18:36 UTC

OP

Is there anyone who thinks that this shit is going to end well?

Early replies (first 3)

61924371
>he isn't looping STRC
ngmi

61924448
>>61924371
Holy shit is finally the end of Bitcoin...

61924465
>>61924170
Strategy's debt is structured quite defensively. Almost all social media commentary completely misses the point because those lazy asses don't even look into how the company an its products work.
STRC in particular pays an adaptable, voluntary dividend. If this product is going to become more popular it's automatically going much lower, maybe more like 5%, but given its voluntary nature and Strategy's opportunism, even if it isn't received well they could also do something like reduce to 3% or 0% and buy back shares to keep the $100 peg instead of depending on the dividend.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61924471
>>61924170
it's a scam and ponzi scheme don't buy it (I just bought more in my Roth today)

61924481
>>61924465
Is a turbo ponzi.

61926067
>>61924170
Saylor is going to have 1m+ BTC by the time all the banks start begging customers to custody with them.

Saylor is going to be the richest guy in the world.
He's never selling, only buying, forever

61926172
or i could just buy btc and btci

61926212
Do you hear that? That's the sound of future wholecoiners being vaporized

61926292
>>61924371
unironically LUNA 2.0 but worse

61926299
>>61924465
>buyback the shares
if you're suggesting they sell btc to do this you know this is how a deathspiral happens

61926327
>>61924170
good thread, what's the options play here

61926359
>>61924170
The big takeaway should be about how long banks have been fucking people with low interest rates on deposits

61926596
>>61926359
Anybody with two brain cells can finda a HYSA or buy a bond ETF

61928120
>>61924170
Strategy (formerly MicroStrategy) keeps the STRC (Stretch) Variable Rate Series A Perpetual Preferred Stock distribution around 10% (ranging between 9% and 11.5% as of early 2026) primarily by utilizing a variable, monthly adjusted dividend rate designed to keep the security trading close to its $100 par value.
The company employs several active management mechanisms to maintain this stability and yield:
Monthly Dividend Adjustment: The Board of Directors adjusts the dividend rate monthly based on the five-day volume-weighted average price (VWAP) of the stock. If the stock price dips below $100 (e.g., during high volatility in Bitcoin), the company increases the dividend to make it more attractive to buyers and pull the price back to par.
At-the-Market (ATM) Offerings: Strategy uses an ATM program to issue new STRC shares, generally targeting a narrow band of $99–$101. This allows them to manage liquidity and support the price, with a $4.2 billion ATM program in place as of late 2025.
Bitcoin-Backed Overcollateralization: Each share of STRC is backed by Bitcoin at a ratio of roughly 5-to-1 (roughly $5 of BTC for every $1 of annual dividend). This immense collateral provides a cushion that reassures investors of the safety of the dividend, allowing for a lower required yield to maintain investor confidence.
Redemption/Call Rights: Strategy has the option to redeem (call) STRC shares at a set price (e.g., $101 per share plus accrued dividends) if the price rises significantly above par, preventing it from trading too high, or as part of active portfolio management.
Cash Buffer: To ensure consistent, high-yield payouts without needing to sell their Bitcoin treasury during down markets, Strategy has established a cash buffer, such as the $1.44 billion reserve announced in late 2025, specifically to fund preferred dividends

61928155
This shit has never worked, ever. It will crash and burn.

61928309
>>61926299
You might be a nice guy, but you're precisely the typical lazy ass commentator I was referring to.
In addition to
>selling bitcoin
they
>already have a cash reserve covering 2y worth of obligations
>can top it up by diluting common stock
>can top it up by selling more STR products
>can top it up by putting out more convertible notes, etc
So if you had ever looked in what they do you'd they can stomach another 1-2 years of bear market without doing anything at all, and then they have 3 fallbacks to generate more cash, and finally they can also sell bitcoin, which due to the cylical nature of this asset may not be a terrible idea either. Sometimes you sell, sometimes you buy, MSTR tries never to sell, but they are extremely opportunistic and can sell at an opportune time if is necessary to do this.

61928376
>>61928309
The fear around them selling as something catastrophic is exaggerated. If you boast of of being over collateralized, selling the collateral has to be an option on the table and as you pointed out they have several options before they'd have to sell to cover dividends.

61928415
>>61928376
Yes, and even if they do sell, they can do a single transaction to cover a year or so of obligations.
This might move the market -2% or so in the process, then -9% when they announce what they did and the inevitable FUD around MSTR selling takes hold, but the market will then have another year or so to calm down and forget about it until the next sale becomes necessary.
The MtGox and Germany government BTC dumps didn't make a sustained dent either, and those were higher amounts and weeks of FUD.

61928472
>>61928309
>already have a cash reserve covering 2y worth of obligations
thats not enough to buy $8bil strc back
>can top it up by diluting common stock
scenario strc unpegged i dont forsee mstr being above NAV
>can top it up by selling more STR products
obviously wouldnt work if strc is spiralling

im not saying its gonna happen soon im just saying a strc deathspiral can happen, his only realistic option to save it is selling btc but we both know thats gigabearish too

61928656
>>61928472
You're already backpedaling to saying it "can" happen.
You didn't address the idea of using a cash reserve that can be built up in good times. It took them a few weeks of mainly diluting the common stock (without much of a price impact) to build their current 2b+ reserve.
Even if they were to top it up using bitcoin sales they could likely also keep the duration of selling and price impact minimal.
>buy $8bil strc back
It's below $4b now. If they go higher, they collect fresh funds in the process, and the product becomes more established and the dividend probably goes down.
OP complained that they raised it to 11.5%, but the stock was already near $100, so they probably just raised it opportunistically to collect more money this week because they want to use the current low prices to buy more. They could likely also have reduced it to 9 or 10% without impacting the price much in the other direction.
In any case, any payments are voluntary and can be suspended/postponed or stopped entirely.
Yes, doing so would hurt the product, but the doomsday nonsense of an imagined necessity to panic-sell into a death spiral simply fails to acknowledge all options.
You can tell Strategy spends all day every day designing a bitcoin treasury and bear commenters don't.

The one thing that's true is that bitcoin halfway needs continue succeeding as a viable asset.
Whether that is in typical 4y cycle or supercycle style or something smoother.
If you think it can go to 0, every treasury company fails, but assuming that bitcoin is going to do at least halfway OK is very premise of buying any treasuries at all.

61928745
>>61924170
no thanks

61929542
>>61928656
bitcoin doesnt need to go anywhere near 0 for mstr to be fucked, we could crab mostly between $50k and $80k for the next 4 years and thats not even bearish

61930030
>>61926212
It's unfathomable for low-IQ no-coiners.
The STRC bitcoin printer is hoovering up 100's of bitcoin per hour of trading in a bear market. With the MSTR ATM going brrrrr alongside, Strategy will have over a million btc before the end of this year, possibly by Q3.
Feeling comfy rn in my ponzi onesie.

61930347
>>61929542
You just made that up like everyone who comments on MSTR because you're lazy. Obviously they'll be fine crabbing 4 years in that range.
The Germany + MtGox dump of 2024 showed that those entities were easily able to dump 110k coins on the market, much of it with ridiculous unplanned market selling, without making a dent for more than a month.
Strategy can likely easily pull 5-10b from bitcoin sales plus shares dilution in a few weeks without causing a lasting crash and it will be enough for many more years in addition to the 2 they alread have.

61930648
>>61926596
hSYA don’t even beat real inflation you goyboy

61930696
>>61930347
> easily able to dump 110k coins on the market without making a dent
and pray tell me, how much did saylor buy in 2024 -now you see the problem?

61930820
>>61930696
Just stop making up random nonsense.
But the fact that nearly 100% of anti-MSTR posts are nonsense like yours makes me quite bullish.
On the way up we'll be able to tell you opposite nonsense and you have no way of validating it and have to accept it. MSTR to acquire Nvidia. It's true because we say so you have to buy the top.

61930877
>>61924170
Yes.
Me.
This is literally free money as long as you sell before Bitcoin goes up by a lot again.
Saylor can see this going as long as he wants. Keeping the price around 95-100
9-11% return on capital
In order for it to cause you trouble, Bitcoin price would have to fall deeper than anyone would expect, which it won't because of that.

61930905
You know how Anons used to go stable coins? This is better. Much better.

61930943
>>61930877
>>61930905
There's something for everyone here, especially its function as tradfi gateway is underappreciated.
When MSTR was still into selling corporate bonds, they got lots of large banks and insurances to buy them. Because the bond market is huge, and bond asset managers found it interesting to buy a bond that moves like bitcoin.
STRC can pull a lot of funds from tradfi money markets as well. Steady, tax-free returns every month, growing history of confirmed ~$100 peg.
Then there's the stock of course.
And many cycle believers who still move a few billions might sit out the bear market in STRC instead of USDT to earn yield.

61930987
>>61930943
> who still move a few billions might sit it out the bear market in STRC instead of USDT
They do. And not only the big ones.

item.title: Day 1650 of snailposting every day until BTC is at 1M — 17 replies — 68.0/hr

market cap?

61920281

61921721

The phrase is "with pleasure" you ESL

61925435

61927063

n
a
i
l

61927377

61927483
BoboSupportsTheAntiSnailMumenRider

61930036

crapto is dead

61930813

61930898

Here's your 2/27: >>61913899
>Day 1650
Here's your 3/01: >>61922023

Here's your 3/02: >>61927118
>Day 1652

No 2/28 in existence, it should have been 1651 in your sequence but instead you made that post on 3/01.

61930971
day 1 of snail bumping until the log is filled with nothing but snailposting

item.title: "Networking" is a waste of time — 11 replies — 44.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
"Networking" is a waste of time

Open thread • Replies: 11 • Updated: 2026-03-03 18:31 UTC

OP

> If you try to network with the rich, they'll laugh at you and go back to their ballgame "DON'T TALK ABOUT WORK PLEASE!"
> Try to network within your friends or family, "Yeah sure I know such and such and they'll let you know!" and you never hear anything back again
> Go to job fairs; "THANK YOU FOR YOUR RESUME! *shakes your hand* YOU CAN APPLY ONLINE AT WWW....NEXT!!!"

Where's the REAL advice on how to make money for once, outside of the memes "get a job" (which btw there are no real entry level jobs anymore) or "just gamble bro" (and end up in the 90%+ lost money statistic).

Sigh.

Early replies (first 3)

61930524
>>61930491
You join forces with other 4channers at a meetup in Miami.

61930527
>>61930491
true

61930539
>>61930491
anon, networking is a euphemism for "sucking cock". You want a job but have little to no experience? go to a bingo night for the elderly. Find the most homosexual old boomer faggot you can find and flirt with him. Be clear adn direct: I'll suck your cock for a job or penetrate you at your whim. Please. Then you land the job. You never noticed Bingo nights are full of 20 year old femboys flirting with old men. Of coarse not, you won't be asking retarded questions about networking here otterwise

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61930630
>>61930491
As head of recruitment, I don't bother with interviewing potential employees. I throw them into a trial networking event and observe how they react. Stiff, stuffy, scared? No thanks. If you don't finish the event energised, at least a dozen business cards in your hand, you're not the candidate for us.

61930680
>>61930491
Networking is for students and extroverts. Go to higher education and go to all the parties. The old boy network is real.

61930745
>>61930630
Why even LARP about this like what’s your reasoning?

61930769
>>61930524
tell me more

61930781
>>61930630
stfu u fake the way to win a networking event is fine one company there that has one or two people who are interested in you. but networking job fairs etc are all scams. the only way in is to lie on your resume like everyone else.

61930871
>>61930491
Networking is great if you're hot. I'm very attractive, athletic and can phone in charisma if I need to (Im socially awkward otherwise).

the rub is you need to be fit, dress well and have an aura to yourself. Want women? Be hot. Want money? Be hot. It's that simple, just be attractive. I think everyone has some control over this in some sense. You need charisma, but you can get away with having less of it if you're hotter but if you have both you will see lots of benefits. That has been my experience. I hate networking but I want to be rich so I need to get back on the horse and get out there again...

61930897
>>61930524
Im in Miami there's no one there who knows anything about this place

61930957
>>61930769
>>61930897
I just think there should be a secret meetup of 4channers at a Hilton in Miami. Pick the date, but don't give a time or exact meetup point. Then have a special color or code to signal you are a fellow 4channer.

Then we can truly discuss business and start building an empire.

the only THEY can realistically sell is food shelter and fertile virgins

item.title: Food is so cheap it is practically free. — 106 replies — 155.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
Food is so cheap it is practically free.

Open thread • Replies: 106 • Updated: 2026-03-03 17:56 UTC

OP

The image posted is in AUD, but for those in the US, Lets assume an hourly wage of $15.

$15 USD in the US can buy 25lbs of rice. which is 45,000 calories. that is astounding.

I work minimum wage (AUS) and after 2 hours of work I can afford an entire weeks worth of food.

the first hour affords me actually nearly a months worth of carbohydrate energy (rice, pasta and potato's)

The second hour affords me 3kg of meat. which is more than enough protein for a week.

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Staple_food

very helpful link to understand there is actually not that much food in the entire world to eat for bulk calories/nutrition. there is about 7 different foods to eat. (wheat, rice, corn, potato, meat, dairy and bananas/plantains in certain regions, honorable mention for beans and lentils as well.)

Early replies (first 3)

61926835
People think they're too good for rice and beans, supplemented with fresh vegetables. They deserve to be poor. For the price of two colas you can buy an entire bag of beans which will feed you for weeks. Stop eating corposlop made in a lab that's marked up 10000%, eat real food.

61926840
>>61926822
>eating is so cheap when you eat like a 13th century

61926847
>>61926822
>food is so heckin cheap when you eat like a medieval Chinese peasant!
>updoots to the left!

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61927442
>>61927417
Well to play semantics, when you say muscle meat, you are talking about the meat, the contractile tissue, fat is separate from this. as they are two different things, but sure. meat (muscle meat, but specifically liver and kidneys) definitely is necessary as it is the largest provider of nutrients by far.

61927498
>>61927022
You are a nigger and a jew.

61927603
>>61926835
Not to mention, if you're gonna waste your dopamine receptors, you might as well be creampie-ing hookers on ecstasy instead of eating cheeseburgers.

61927622
>>61926835

Based. Eating rice, beans and scrambled eggs is peak. Poorfags be cuck'd for the mc'ds tho. Wondering why they poor n tummy still upset. Sad.

61927677
>>61926822
Switched to keto 3 days ago.
I'm done with carbslop.
Rice and beans alone are mestizo feed and give you long term health issues.
Surprisingly it isn't costing me more than $5 a day, but I tend to buy things on discount.

61927697
food was absurdly cheap pre covid. 12 dollar lunch buffets. the 1 dollar mcchicken. sausage meats starting at 1 dollar per lb. that shit was so cheap i was buying them to feed to my raccoons.

now its more expensive but the basics are still pretty good. minimum wage can buy 2-4 dozen eggs per hour.

61927747
>>61926840
>>61926847
Rice with chicken and beans is infinitely more nutritious than Mcdonalds. Cry about it you are weak. Cheap food cooked well is the most beneficial.

61927938
>>61926835
>rice and beans, supplemented with fresh vegetables
this and tuna/deenz+salad is top tier cuisine
As a student I survived so well on this. Best food ever.

61928034
>>61927193
Soil has no very little to do with nutrients. Genetics of the plant has more control over nutrients, so the long lasting bland food that was bred to be that way is the problem. Having regional markets and actual seasons for strains of vegetables that taste good and don't have a long shelf life would contribute to having more nutrients in food.

61928068
>>61926848
Mountain Dew

61928075
>>61926822
>>61926835
Holy fuck. Irredeemably delusional.
Eating only rice and beans like one day you will wake up and have a meltdown about why you did that stupid overly frugal living shit to yourself. That is not gonna be a good day.

61928215
>>61926822
the chemicals and fake meat in Mcmuttslop are literal poison, at least in europe they force these evil fucks to make real food

61928231
>>61926822
Okay great, doesn't matter though when rent is your largest expense by a huge margin

61928338
>>61926867
fpbp

61928341
>>61926867
6 billion pajeets and gooks suggest otherwise

61928406
>>61926822
>calories

Amazing maths there. I better start drinking sunflower oil instead of eating food - that has twice the calories of rice! Just 11lbs of that already beats your 45000 calories bag of rice. Astounding

61928427
>>61926835
>vegetables
anything that isn't potatoes/carrot/cabbage/onion is expensive

61928442
>>61926822
>>61926835
>>61926870
Based. Most first world people just don't have any frugal skills. They fall for the consumer slop and blame there low wage for being broke.

61928596
>>61928075
You don't need to eat beans and rice, you can eat egg rice with minced meat, that's cheap as shit too. Or something like potato with fried chicken. Or Caesar salad. All things you can easily make yourself that cost very little

61928700
>>61926822
McDonalds is garbage
Rice has no nutrition

61928746
>>61927442
Fair enough if you want me to be completely specific, because it really impossible to consume muscle meat without consuming fat, it is always connected unless you are a freak that cuts it off.
But to concede semantics, you need the muscle meat and associated fat, in a ratio of around 70-30 more or less respectively. Organs aren't required, they are nutritious, sometimes overly nutritious to a toxic level in excess.
I don't eat them mainly because they taste nasty. My bones aren't disintigrating, my muscles still grow stronger through training, my shits are 100 times better than before and I have no debilitating chronic diseases. N =1 but it just works.
To keep it /biz/ related, this diet is incredibly cheap and simple and still satiating and appetizing, and I can't recommend it enough. It won't be as cheap as beans and rice but also you won't be sick, constipated, low energy, brain fogged, skinny fat grain slave that ends up spending 200k in hospital bills and medicines.
I spend $400 a month on food, but I do get the much more expensive grass fed ground beef and special cuts from a local farm because I can afford it. If you are in a pinch, those 70/30 beef tubes from walmart could easily get you to survive with minimal effort and probably only $150 a month or so.

61928773
>>61928700
Rice is ok. I wasted way too much of my life studying biochemistry.

Its kinda shit to have incomplete proteins with low bioavailability. You want to have like 1 gram per pound of bodyweight of protein per day. Ideally Cysteine & Taurine.

Look into the cuts of meat we stopped eating in like the late 1800s/early 1900s, these ones are cheap and actually have what we need

61928775
>>61926835
>People think they're too good for rice and beans,
yes, I am rich.

61928813
>>61926822
it's never been easier to avoid eating slop because as well as raising the prices so much food companies also have degraded the quality of ingredients in many snacks and processed foods so they don't taste good anymore.

61928855
>>61927677
retard, carbs are key for energy and recovery, you just have to learn to eat the right amount at the right time, 100g rice 2-3 hours before lifting, 40g rice after lifting to restore glycogen stores, lower carbs at dinner, etc…

61928879
>>61928855
>retard, carbs are key for energy and recovery,
(in your opinion)
after a few months of adaptation, 0 carb btfo carb loading in virtually every single scenario, along with other added benefits. Carbs are just trash. Even animals that eat only plants like cows convert their food into fatty acids through fermentation.

61928887
>>61928773

My go to lunch bowl is rice + eggs + peas (or something else). Very cheap and basically macronutritionally complete.

61928919
>>61926822
>t. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=_pDTiFkXgEE

61930720
Feds on 4chan teaching chuds how to cook to prevent collapse wasn't on my 2026 bingo card

61930744
For me, it's oats. They contain:
>Magnesium (muscle and nerve function)
>Iron (oxygen transport)
>Zinc (immune health)
>B vitamins (energy metabolism)
>Manganese (bone formation, metabolism)
>Protein (more than most grains)
>Contains avenanthramides (unique antioxidants in oats)

item.title: Does IQ make you more successful financially? — 43 replies — 73.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
Does IQ make you more successful financially?

Open thread • Replies: 43 • Updated: 2026-03-03 18:02 UTC

OP

Does IQ make you more successful financially?

Early replies (first 3)

61927833
>>61927814
No, but emotional intelligence does.

61927872
>>61927833
>emphaty
retard detected

61927878
>>61927814
>>61927833
>>61927872


now post her without make up and filters and 0.1sec clips

i'll wait

fucking yellow fever fags

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61928387
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Emotional_intelligence

61928394
What if somebody wants to learn the latin language and shit. Maybe they font want to optimize the tiktok.

61928410
>>61927814
Yes, everything else is cope and lies of low IQ people who really want to be high IQ and tells that kind of lies to them selfs.

61928414
Asian pussy makes you successful. That's why WMAF is the future.

61928426
>>61927833
>Motivation
All is vanity as the preacher said. I can do things though but my heart will not be in any of them
>Social skills
Yes, i have them but i hate people and choose to avoid them like the plague
>Empathy
Real intelligence entails high levels of empathy. I get you're sad but i won't cry with you because your cat has died. I don't expect you to cry if my father dies. I won't. It's the circle of life, Simba.
>Self awareness
I can read a room perfectly enough to know when my antisemitic jokes haven't landed as i expected
>Self regulation
Buzzword i don't know what it means. I can regulate and adjust myself according to the situation.

61928448
>>61927814
I have ~130IQ and i'm a poorfag NEET college dropout at 24. I have very little self-control or motivation so it's hard to get and hold a job, and I am very socially awkward so it's hard to make connections (the only way to get any skilled job nowadays), so i ended up an outcast. Many people in that range seem to have similar issues.

However, if you have 110-120IQ you probably will be "normal" enough to not be a weird outcast and smart enough to get ahead of most normalfags, and if you're >145IQ you're a genius and will probably end up being world-class at something.

I'm not trying to sadpost or get sympathy, it's just what I've observed.

61928455
>>61928448
Yea being king of the midwits is the best, you just crash out if you have le intelligence and realize we live in hell

61928672
>>61927833
Every billionaire is a psycho that eats the babies he just raped and the goyim be like
>duuuude just be nice and money will come to you :)

61928885
>>61928448
I'm sorry man. Whenever I meet academic people they seem to be not that bright. Often they can only do that one thing they are specialized and that's it. They just had that motivation to study hard but they are not that smart. Still they make make good money. They just fit into the system and profit from it.

61928893
>>61927814
IQ is simply the ceiling for skill and knowledge. It alone doesn't guarantee anything.

61929003
>>61928885
That's because academic people *aren't* that bright. The median IQ of college professors is only 128, only slightly higher than medical doctors (124).
>>61928448
I have a 161 IQ and I'm also a college dropout.
>if you're >145IQ you're a genius and will probably end up being world-class at something
I wouldn't say I'm world-class at anything except shitposting on 4chan, but I think the extra IQ means it's easy to do well without really trying. The place I was working had spent years and millions of dollars trying to solve some knotty problems and had made no progress despite having a PhD and several mensa members on the team. I happened to overhear some discussion about it in the lunchroom and before I finished my ham sandwich I had figured out the solution, which led to me getting promoted to the technical lead on that team and making pretty good money, a decade later I'm in the top 1% in net worth in the US, all for a whopping 5 minutes of actual thinking.

61929120
IQ doesn't exist.

The only thing it tests, is if you are cognitively retarded or not. But then again, basically any type of test can determine that.

The only reason people still talk about IQ is because people's fragile ego's and political dogmas hinge upon it. Not because it has any scientific merit.

61929203
>>61929120
>reeee this thing I don't have doesn't ackshually exist
It's funny because you think you're being clever about it, but it's all just so transparent and sad.

61929223
>>61929203
It's funny how you confirmed exactly what he said with your little emotional outburst.
I have no idea what my IQ is myself, never tested it. All the smart guys I know never tested their IQ either, if you care about it, that's already a sign you're an insecure midwit

61929256
>>61927814
how the fuck would i know

61929257
>>61929120
t. 94 IQ faggot who thought he was the next Einstein before taking the test

61929269
>>61929223
Actual smart guys were taken by their parents to a psychologist for IQ testing in grade school though, it's a necessary step for skipping grades and early graduation/college.

61929405
>>61929257
>>61929203
This is always the boring predictable response from losers and failures with no achievements in life, and no real talents. Just to bait me into revealing that I happened to score very highly on the IQ test.

Lack of introspection is a sign of low intelligence though. So naturally when you got *your* bullshit score, you didn't question it for a second, and proudly displayed it on your reddit profiles.

61929628
>>61929405
>So naturally when you got *your* bullshit score, you didn't question it for a second
this is evidence you never did an IQ test in your life

61929644
>>61929269
Why are you trying to proof he had no high IQ? You are proofing him again by doing it. "people's fragile ego's and political dogmas hinge upon it." :)

61929692
>>61927814
does pooping make women smell better?

61929814
Get a load of this young lady.
(temperance)

61930178
>>61927872
Its a jeet.
>hello saars pleas have the emphaty

61930196
>>61927878
she is not fat.
i simple cannot get hard with fat women.
even an average asian woman is 10x hotter than your average burger woman by virtue of not being disgustingly obese.

61930320
>>61928672
I'm opportunist and being nice pays off most of the time. They are ways to make money that require being asshole but being an asshole alone doesn't make any money. Some people always want to play against the rules but that's sailing against the wind and makes things unnecessary difficult. Most men are not willing to please a man to get rich. It doesn't need to be homoerotic. Just understanding what HE wants and giving it to him in exchange to the thing you want from him. It can be anything. Something simple as being nice or doing him a favor. Since I have a boyfriend I have learned a lot about men. They are just as emotional as women but try to hide it all the time. If you can read them anyway you know weather they like or dislike something you do. You can read if they are emotionally attached to you, to know if you can ask them for a favor.

Bullying or trolling people on 4chan doesn't make any money but people do it anyway because they want to feel good. They don't think about weather each of there actions in life will cost or gain them money long term. There only measurement is weather it feels good in the moment.

61930333
>>61927833
sounds gay

61930366
>>61927881
There is a difference between empathy and compassion. If you don't have empathy you are kind of retarded.

61930381
>>61930366
^^^

61930579
>>61927833
All five of these are so critical that lacking one can ruin your entire life. It's the term "emotional intelligence" that's really gay and ruins the whole thing. Swapping the word emotional for something like behavioral or social would be more well received.

61930771
>>61930579
That's a male problem and not the words fault. It's job is to be true, not to be seen as straight and be popular among men.

PYPL merger edition 1

Open thread • Replies: 88 • Updated: 2026-03-03 16:56 UTC

OP

Here we go!

Early replies (first 3)

61891698
Do it Ryan.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61904108
I bought the pico bottom but only threw in $1000

61904711
>>61904697
HOLY FUUUUCK
OOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOO

61904727
>>61891742
>Ripple Labs will buy PayPal. Screen Cap this.
I don't think so, only because they claimed they didn't have any big acquisitions planned for the rest of the year

61908061
>>61903001
739M SHARE

61909665
no merger.
we are fucked

61909693
>>61909665
the gap will be filled

61909824
>>61904697

61910089
>>61906351
Thanks for playing, baggies.

61910124
>>61909824

61915327
Any news?

61916113
>>61915327
https://www.marketbeat.com/instant-alerts/filing-intech-investment-management-llc-purchases-30734-shares-of-paypal-holdings-inc-pypl-2026-02-28/

61918275
Is this still going to 60$? I went all in at 46 because of this thread.

61919240
>>61918275
I Hope return at price of institutional have buy

61919338
>>61892826
>$300
impressive

61919350
>>61918275
>d
the CEO will get his big cut only at $83 dollar share price MINIMUM.
look at warner bros that shit went from below $10 to now beeing baught at $30
If Paypal gets aquired it will be atleast $100

61919773
>>
https://hedgefollow.com/stocks/PYPL
Hope

61921315
>>61919350
I doubt it will go that high.

61923340
>>61923318

61923342
>>61923340

61925981
>>61904727
At the rate they were going, it wasn't going to be a big acquisition, merely a ripple on the pond

61926795
AMZN

61927102
Can someone explain why Paula even exists?? I remember you needed it for eBay back when everything was new. Somehow it morphed into the default asking to payment

61928616
>>61927102
It exists because easy cc payment processor services didn't exist for a long time. You used to give your cc info directly to the web vendors at payment, which eventually got people scammed on shady websites. Paypal was the first of its kind there. The internet has somehow COMPLETELY forgotten that money used to be a complete pain in the ass to move around. Btc, being somewhat clunky now, was actually a huge move in being able to transfer money without oversight or other friction overhead. Now that its been incorporated into the collectivist panopticon, both btc and PayPal are harder to use than just years ago

61930361
ooooooooooooooooooooooooooo

item.title: I do love how boomers, because of their calcified brains, just keep on dumping money into housing because it's a "safe i… — 30 replies — 34.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
I do love how boomers, because of their calcified brains, just keep on dumping money into housing because it's a "safe i…

Open thread • Replies: 30 • Updated: 2026-03-03 17:45 UTC

OP

I do love how boomers, because of their calcified brains, just keep on dumping money into housing because it's a "safe investment".

When they started buying housing in the 1970s, the land to structure value ratio was 1 : 4, now it's reversed and is like 3 :1 in the opposite direction. A $800,000 property has like $250,000 in the structure and the other $550,000 is the value of the land itself. But when you plug in the 1970s ratio it's like $70,000 for the land. And you can't say it's because land is scarce, because India is much more crowded and their land is worth fuck all.

Housing prices here have crashed 30% in real terms since Covid and oblivious boomers STILL think "housing only goes up" and are dumping their remaining money into real estate. The only reason I think the prices have crashed to the degree they have is because a lot of boomers literally died off or ended up in the rocking chair.

They apparently got traumatized by a stock market crash in the 1980s and since then have been risk adverse to any investment that isn't real estate. Which is fucking stupid. Real estate is literally a DEPRECIATING ASSET. How fucking retarded do you have to be to assume it'll just keep on doubling in value forever.

A lot of the housing they're living in is completely fucked too. Old ladies that live from 65-85 functionally stop doing repairs in the last ten years of their life, so by the end you're just left with a moldy rotting mess.

Early replies (first 3)

61928209
>buy modest home for $125k in 2019
>have over $200k in PMs in 2026
If my home goes to $25k, that means others in the neighborhood are doing the same. I'll just buy more houses and rent them out.

61928240
I live in a house that requires two college graduate salaries. Even the most industrious beaner with a lawn care company can't afford to be my neighbor. I haven't heard Spanish in 6+ months unless it's from someone I have paid to be there to do something menial.
I don't even remember what black people look like

61928243
The internet is filled with financially illiterate zoomers who don't understand what inflation is.

>Inflation is down, then why are things still expensive
>Housing prices have gone up through out all of human history, but in 2026 it's going stop going up because some brown incel on /pol/ said so

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61928246
Even if the value of a boomer's house went up 2% a year, that doesn't offset the time and cost of mowing the lawn every week.

61928250
>>61928192
historically it does go up, doesn't mean there aren't peaks and crashes

61928265
in my retarded fucking cunt, sPAIN, we are no longer building new buildings while importing millions of immigrants each year.
i dont think housing prices are going down any time soon, theres infinite demand and zero supply.

61928281
>>61928265
Those thirdies will, after a certain point, just not comply to western building standards and regulations, and start living in literal trailers and shacks on land. They're already compressing 6 of them into a bedroom like sardines, that'll just get worse.

Already in Canada you're starting to see actual encampments being built on Public land, with the government basically giving up on clearing them. That's how the Brazilian Favelas got their start.

61928328
>>61928243
>All of human history
Wrong. Housing was fucking expensive in England in the 1500s, but in the 1600s it was as cheap as permission to join a charter to the New World. Housing was fucking expensive in the 1930s but post war saw housing go down thanks to mass housing projects. Prices fluctuate. Homes aren't any different.

61928639
>>61928281
>indian favelas located on public streets
Trudeau will eventually be remembered as the grim reaper of canada. But he got a Freemason slave family out of it, so maybe it was worth it to him

61928662
>>61928192
You can either play the game or keep whining about those that do and lose.

61928666
>>61928246
Mowing is fun though

61928675
This is adjusted for inflation, London is so fucked lmao, turns out no one wants to live with foreigners

61928694
>>61928675
Oh and you could see this as a good correction since gdp per capita has been stagnant for like 20 years so it is good that 20 years of growth in house prices has been wiped out (though gotta suck for the sellers that have held for a long time and have to pay capital gains tax when they havent earned anything in real terms lmao)

61928723
/biz only seethes at homes so much because of sour grapes

any brown retard can download an app and buy $50 of a memecoin. but to buy a house you either need tons of cash or a good job and good credit. you can't buy $50 worth of house

the neets, jeets, and larpers of /biz/ are quite literally priced out

61928731
>>61928675
That's just rich people fleeing UK because of new taxes or something

61928736
>>61928723
Young people also hate the idea of being tied to one place. But by the time they move from one place to another about 10 times, they'll want to get off the rentie carousel and settle down with a nice house

61928766
>>61928736
Zoomies can do math Boomer, owning a house is a crappy investment right now. My rent is cheaper than my insurance + property taxes + amortized interest and that's with a 150k downpayment (that most people don't have). I have that 150k in the market and it made 13% last year between equities and CDs/Bonds, houses in my area went up 0.7% yoy, they literally lost value relative to inflation.

61928786
>>61928766
At the same time, you’re renting. What do you get in the end? A pat on the back? With buying you actually have something.

61928794
>>61928786
His argument is that he "gets" the return on his investments that would otherwise be tied up in the house. It makes sense, but probably only applies in certain areas. At least where I live, mortgage payments are lower than rent.

61928820
>>61928240
/thread

61928835
>>61928766
>made 13% last year
And how much did rent go up? Substract that from your profits, retard. You will keep getting gradually priced out of renting every year. Renting never gets cheaper. Even when housing gets cheaper, renting somehow doesn't

61928903
>>61928731
Obersturmführer Keir Starmer took office in july 2024, it was crashing well before that

61928911
>>61928835
Supply and demand actually works on rents too

61929054
>>61928192
Home prices aren't coming down, rates aren't either. You waited for a repeat of something isn't going to happen again

61929310
>>61928835
>And how much did rent go up?
So you acknowledge that i am making a profit relative to buying? I'm on a month to month lease, rents actually went down in my local area but I'm the retard still paying the same 2023 prices instead of leaving my rented duplex. Say how are your property taxes trending?

>>61928786
>At the same time, you’re renting. What do you get in the end? A pat on the back? With buying you actually have something.
Have what a larger property taxes bill? Enjoy getting scammed by your local government for an "asset". I wonder how much loss the housing market will suffer with restricted immigration and millions of dying boomers whose heirs will dump their three houses onto the market. With renting, I have growth in my equities and CDs, if I realized just last years gains I'd have almost 20k for doing nothing, that's like double your salary.

61929671
>be me
>the prophet has spoken
>life is meaningless without Keys and Za
>mortgage? eternal debt? irrelevant
>what matters is holding the sacred ring of Keys in one hand and the greasy Disc of Za in the other
>ancestors built cathedrals, empires, dynasties
>I will build equity… maybe… after 27 years of payments
>but today I ascend, for today I post Keys and Za on plebbit
>thousands of upvotes, dopamine sacrament, communion in tomato sauce
>I am complete, I am saved, I am His disciple

hail Keys, all hail Za

61929841
>>61928192
Sounds like cope. I got a house 2 years ago with zero down payment, and a mortgage with $150 lower monthly payments than I used to rent a shitty studio for. 2.15% APR. Feels comfy, I already built up almost 100k in equity by paying less than rent.

61929982
OP has no idea what he is talking about.
First off, the boomers who were invested in stocks made bank in the 1980s, even though there was that short lived crash in 87. it was the dot.com bubble crash in 2001 that spooked them.

Secondly, the tax benefits of real estate are significant and the leverage is key.
>buy $500,000 house
>put $50k cash in as DP
>house goes up in value by 5% every year.
>pay $30k every year in PITI
>year 1 ~RoC = 31%
>at the end of ten years, home value is $825k
>you have put $350k into it
>all of your interest was tax deductable
> so over 10 years, you saved ~$40k in taxes
>10 year return on cash is 104%
>you also got to live in it, so didn't pay rent
>10 years of rent at avg $3000/month = $360,000
>Adjusted 10 yr RoC = 207%
And if you sell it, the proceeds are tax free, increasing the value of the investment at sale by 30% or more.

Now do it as an investment:
>$350k rental house = 20% DP = $70k cash
>assume net even cash flow year 1; rents increase 5%/yr, so rent 2400/mo Year 1
>costs stay relatively stable year over year (mortgage stays the same, but Insurance; taxes; repairs increase 5%/yr
>yr 2: rent = $2520; costs = $2441
>yr 2 cashflow = +$950
>end of yr 2 value = $385,875
>RoC = 52%
>yr 10 rent = $3900/month
>yr 10 expenses = $2900/mo
>yr 10 value = $570k
>cumulative 10 year cashflow (income) = $60,000 (tax deferred bc of "depreciation")
>Loan principal paid by renters = $70,000
>10 year total return = $350k
>10 yr RoC = 500%
>if you sell, about 50% of proceeds will be taxed at a reduced LT Capital Gains rate, netting you an additional $17k

61930144
>>61928675
You can literally found a "clean streets" party and gather the hearts of millions of voters. No left, no right, just genocide.

61930676
>>61928192
These rent shills are just communists shilling for their WEF/Mosiach agenda
>you will own nothing and be happy
What a (((coincidence))) that this fits one of the requirements for the jewish antichrist to come.

Cope you filthy whore and write you rent threads while I invite your mother to come over. No you are not allowed to do the same since your landLORD doesn't allow people to come over. Kek.

61922014

61922070

61922078

61922734
No one is buying toad in a shib thread! Shib is going to 14x to ath and toad will go to zero. Shib already has an etf coming and toad has desperate curry flavored shills. LMAO!

61923250
>>61922734
When toad moons YOU'RE going to seethe, when toad moons YOU'RE going to rope!

61923260
>>61899833
I am in anon, people are too stupid to skip this one.
And some Doge in case the elom makes a comeback.

61924787
>>61922734
See
>>61922014

61926036
>>61899833
This thread smells like curry and shit.

Also, shibaggies holding their shibags to zero.

61926122
>>61926036
>This thread smells like curry and shit.
So like most threads on /biz ?

>>61926036
>Also, shibaggies holding their shibags to zero.
Sure buddy. The number 1 meme on eth is going to zero goy!

61927655
>>61926122
>The number 1 meme on eth is going to zero goy!
i'm genuinely considering dumping the rest of what i hold because of this post

61927803
>>61927655
Checked dump it

61927812
>>61899833
you will be lucky to get a 3x

61927816
>>61927812
Shib is ez 10x bear case 33x bull case

61927892
>>61927816
Never happening

61928058
>>61927892
>Shib will never go to a place it’s been before because it just won’t.
You sound tarded bro

61928094
>>61928058
It only went there because of insane hype and FOMO. It will never happen again unless Elon makes it the official currency of X or something

61928421
>>61927655
Please do fud chud. I like cheapies.
>>61927812
>>61928094
He didn't buy cheap before the etf and clarity act passed.
>NGMI!

61929970

item.title: /pmg/ Pirates Metals Goblins — 59 replies — 23.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
/pmg/ Pirates Metals Goblins

Open thread • Replies: 59 • Updated: 2026-03-03 16:05 UTC

OP

ARR!

Early replies (first 3)

61926222
yarrr

61926235
>>61926222
arr har har harr! get ye telescope and check this shipmate!

61926254
Arrgh matey, IQCHADJEETs Silva stock bussin nigga frfr on kirk nigga

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61928765
>another gay dip
If you are going to dip, do it to 50$ or don't. I am not buying more "dips" at 80.

61928769
Keep on stacking! I got some 4x 900er silver peace dollars 24,xx g from 1924 and some more few coins in a LCS. (They thin but are huge) I will return, need earn some more money first. ... meanwhile:
As I wrote; Prognose 2026.02.13 for 2026.03.02-03 >>61848331 It happend again just like that.
Not so, funny, facts; Anyone noticed yet that this attack is (almost) in synchronity with this year "Purim" (03.03.2026), also fullmoon+ritualist red moon in some regions.
Also bombing of Jugoslavia was at around Purim 1999.02.28 [Purim for Hebrew Year 5759 began on Monday, 1 March 1999 and ended on Tuesday, 2 March 1999](Fullmoon: Dienstag
2. März 1999, 07:58:30 Uhr),
Iraq 2003.03.20 [Purim for Hebrew Year 5763 began on Monday, 17 March 2003 and ended on Tuesday, 18 March 2003] (Fullmoon: Dienstag
18. März 2003, 11:34:30 Uhr)
and on Ghadaffi/Libia 2011.03.19 (FR)/22 (NATO) [Purim for the year 2011 is celebrated/ observed on sundown of Friday, March 18th ending at sundown on Saturday, March 19th.]
(Fullmoon: Samstag, 19. März 2011, 19:10:06 Uhr) and all on around fullmoon too.
So, just telling, interessting, not? Enjoy the show and be astounished.
There are sure more similar dates/occurencies like the RF Wagner Boss https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yevgeny_Prigozhin and crew died 23.8.2023 in plane bomb/crash on half moon/changing moon day (24.8.2023)
after messed up with RF rulers months earlier.
You might check yourself for more on such. I should have put some bet on this actuall thing, missed opportunity again.
How did I know? Simple by living long enough, experience, and observing patterns and be eager for how mechanics work or not work.
... And what´s up with the China-COMEX demand of silver thing? Nothing happens?

61928790
Stacktards said silver would be $130 today,,,,lmao what is it $70-$75,,,,yes thats what coin shops are offering nigga, on K_rk on G_d

Lesson in that ,,,,,pmg baggies are dumb as fuck,,,,,do the opposite nigga,,,,get rich miga

61928793
OH NO NO NO SILVERSISSIES

61928808
>>61928765
Yeah I'm waiting for a 50 dip to buy again. With how retarded the economy is, I need to start saving fiat again.

61928809
Lord Shortington and Slam Gapore (formally Tam Ping Lo) must have been busy tamping for the blood moon ritual. It went down and faded with the lunar eclipse and bounced back after the moon got out of Earth's shadow. The heavy hitters are usually Tampowitz and Ag Capone. Unless premarket starts at 6AM EST. Then that explains that particularly large dip. Ag Capone, did you get to work at 5AM for a silly occult ritual the same time Shortington was praying to "St." Jimmy Savile next to statues of Gog and Magog? After hours is normally your thing Ag Capone.

61928826
>>61928809
Lord Shortington was the true star today, so far.

61928839
>>61928826
Yall laughed when Petey B shorted at $117 nigga,,,,,called him a joo,,,,who's laughing now nigga?

61928841
>>61928790
It normally creeps up a few dollars a day naturally. Someone would have to buy a year's worth of production at once to get up that fast. Only paper silver pumps and dumps like a shit coin.

61928848
>>61928841
>It normally creeps up a few dollars a day naturally
There are entire decades where it just creeps down, zoom out. If you aren't in the game to have a shiny collection and larp for decades, you're about to be really buttmad for a long time.

61928859
fuck we're not getting out of the boo box today are we

61928867
>>61928826
I would be surprised if the market was open on Purim in any western city. Or was it the shabbos goys in lambskin aprons doing it? Because "Dude! Blood Moon!".

61928870
>>61928841
Paper is cheaper than physical because you have to pay 20% premiums,,,,people who buy physical are broke retards on k_rk

61928874
Afternoon m8ys
Excellent to see the Captain back at the helm.

61928884
>>61928841
>Only paper silver pumps and dumps like a shit coin.

Talking about shitcoins good Ol' Black Rock one of the biggest real estate assets collectors is starting to liquidate its Bitcoin assets. Doesn't seem like it's a good year to be a crypto bro.

61928922
>>61928867
Blood moon, im gonna COOM!

61928930
>>61928870
The premiums are normally lower. When things are unstable they get very high.

>>61928848
I am talking about $10+ pumps and dumps happening in a single day. That is not natural. If this dropped $15 dollars in a month it would be believable. But you can't even do that because of real supply and demand for silver. And everyone can see when the big dumps happen and figure out who is doing it based on time zone. Cartels killing Mexican miners and silver bombs being dropped on Iran is now added onto your cheap silver tamping problems.

61928997
>>61928930
You're forgetting about Jane Street recently becoming one of the biggest Silver Futures holder right now. You know, the same one being investigated by India and China.

https://coinpedia.org/news/why-is-silver-falling-jane-streets-1-3b-slv-bet-sparks-manipulation-debate/
https://www.reuters.com/world/india/jane-street-deposits-567-million-escrow-accounts-per-india-regulatory-directives-2025-07-14/
https://archive.is/wip/dCGDy

Fuckers are even beating Black Rock. Trying to apply technical analysis to this market while fun is ultimately an exercise in futility if you don't know all of its key players suppressing the price. The only good position in Silver is owning it physically.

61929008
>>61928997
>The only good position in Silver is owning it physically.


The choice of broke retards,,,,no thanks shill

61929016
>>61928930
>Cartels killing Mexican miners and silver

The price dropped $20 yall said this would make it go up $30,,,,lmao yall learned nothing and still claim to nostradamus

61929416
>>61929008
Most of us bought years ago. Myself included. (And yes I did sell a few ounces when it was over $80 a while ago.) The rest are people willing to buy at a loss or are retards. I am here to watch for fun. You are not stressing me out. I wait for Comex to be found empty with glee.

61929468
>>61929416
>And yes I did sell a few ounces when it was over $80 a while ago.
absolutely no shame in that. It's quite literally what silver is for. You buy it when the price is low, build up your stack, and once the GSR closes down like it did recently you sell off no more than half of your stack and convert the FIAT you receive into free gold.
Holding silver forever hoping it will make you rich one day is the same as buying bitcoin nowadays and hoping for the same.
Rinse and repeat.

61929486
About to try and get a refund from TD, been 36 business days and no confirmation of my 4 bars being shipped. They’re gonna try and say some final sale bullshit, but they’ve not held up their part of the “contract”. I stupidly grabbed 5 one ounce silver maples 2 weeks ago, might try to lump that in with the refund (x to doubt for this part being refund) cause I could just run and slurp those 5 locally for same price I paid on TD. Inb4 I get the refund and silver moons before I get the cash back in my account to go buy at my LCS

61929507
I considered selling a little silver for gold recently but quite like that Clive Thompson characters approach if at least waiting for 30to one instead of 50plus. It's nice to have a little pike of silver anyway even if I prefer gold. So atm I'll jyst dca more

61929573
>>61929468
Didn't sell my gold. Not buying gold right now. Maybe in a few years I will buy some bling. Waiting for stock market cheapies and pampering myself this month. Thanks occult retards. You are so predictable. It is fun to find out how many of you blackmailed ziontards own shares stocks I still hold.

61929582
>>61929573
please rewrite that in English and without the schizobabble

61929747
>>61929582
People who got rich have multi generational wealth spend money to cause things to fulfil prophesies because they are bored. It works until people catch on and can figure out when they buy and sell stuff. Purim blood moon at 3:33 AM PST? Massive sell off. Sell some of your stocks and before that day especially institutional investor heavy stocks. Then wait for cheapies in things you were eyeing. Just bought a share of an oil stock that dumped. Makes no sense that it would dump when it was showing the start of a long slow upward trajectory before a classic bull run. And that was before the Iran thing. Oil stock are hardly dumping because they shouldn't be. Aware people are buying today while they dump good stocks for no apparent reason because of dumb rituals. I will wait much longer for other things. I know this could go on for years.

61929770
>>61926208
Anyone else remember AYY lmao coin anon?
Had to sell all his coins because of a family emergency like 2 years ago

I still think about this alien coins he had some fucking cool shit
Poor fucker missed the pump

61929916
>>61929486
Anyone ever tried or had luck with refunding from TD precious metals? Shithawking the chart before I send off my email, knowing today it’ll pull some Talmud magic and moon when I ask for a refund/get refunded

61930008
>>61926208
4 March 2027, we meet and see who has the last laugh. You confident?

item.title: WHY DOES SILVER HAVE AIDS??? — 18 replies — 19.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
WHY DOES SILVER HAVE AIDS???

Open thread • Replies: 18 • Updated: 2026-03-03 17:42 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

61928742
>>61928732
>He didn't have a buy order at $80 where the obvious entry is

61928753
jude street

61928754
>>61928732
I called it weeks ago.
Silver trannies too busy fomoing in and were in euphoria.
Easiest sell or my life
>t. Sold at $105
Thanks for holding my bag, take care of it good for the next 3 decades

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61928782
>>61928754
It is likely that this is the 2008 rally which will be exceeded by a higher 2011 rally. So it might be held for awhile but it isn't going to be decades.

61928785
Oh no silversissies not like this ...

61928787
>>61928732
Kek silverbaggies got cocky, again!

61928801
>>61928732
Oil panic. Everybody is switching their money to that. It will come back to silver once the panic settles.

61928805
>>61928782
Not with these gains. This is a big one.

61928816
>>61928782
Silversissies do we REALLY?

61928957
So where's the bottom?

61929887
>>61929018

61930303
>>61929887

61930342
>>61928732

61930373
Trump literally bombed Iran to suppress the fiat price of silver

61930384
>>61928957

61930436
>>61928732
because COMEX has a history of getting fucked in the ass by JPMorgan

61930656
>>61928732
wait until all the miners are spooled up and see what the price crashes to, it's profitable to mine intentionally for silver at $40
